Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for issues under the expense categorizer project to identify prerequisite tickets. Use linear_list_projects (query: "expense" or "categorizer" or similar) to find the project "Expense Auto-Categorization Engine" (project ID: proj_expense_autocategorizer). Then use linear_list_issues (project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or similar) to retrieve all tickets under this project. Discover the following prerequisite fix tickets relevant to relaunch readiness: ENG-214 ("[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config," assigned to Lena Björkström, state: In Progress), ENG-215 ("[P1] Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config," assigned to Lena Björkström, state: In Progress), ENG-217 ("[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline," assigned to Lena Björkström, state: In Progress), ENG-218 ("Dashboard: Add type validation to policy config editor," assigned to Anh Nguyen, state: In Progress), ENG-219 ("Define launch readiness checklist," assigned to Samira Tariq, state: To Do), ENG-212 ("Remaining edge case coverage," assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state: In Progress), and ENG-216 ("[URGENT] Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses," assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state: In Progress). This is a read/lookup action.
OE 2: Read comments on the prerequisite Linear tickets to check for completion signals. Use linear_list_comments (issueId for each relevant issue) to read comments on ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-219, ENG-212, and ENG-216. Discover completion evidence in comments: ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) -- Lena's deploy verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified, attached to ENG-215 / issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) explicitly states "Closing ENG-214" and confirms config corrections deployed and verified in production for both clients; the completion evidence for ENG-214 is found cross-referenced in ENG-215's comment thread rather than on ENG-214 directly. ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) -- Lena's deploy verification comment confirms config corrections deployed and verified: Danielle Osei's chair correctly approved, Andre Washington's desk correctly denied, coworking correctly denied. ENG-217 (Pipeline hardening) -- Dmitri's audit results comment (comment_dmitri_audit_results) is posted on this ticket (47 expenses, 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 total financial exposure); this confirms the audit (ENG-216) is complete but does not itself signal that the pipeline hardening work is done -- no separate completion comment for ENG-217's pipeline fixes is found. ENG-218 (Dashboard type validation) -- no completion comment found on this ticket; still showing as In Progress with no evidence of completion. ENG-212 (Edge cases) -- Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_validation_tests) confirming 6 schema validation tests implemented and passing with corrected configs (test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback); also notes that Lena is incorporating these tests into the ENG-217 pipeline deployment gate. ENG-211 (Pilot monitoring) -- Samira's comment (comment_samira_pause_confirmed) listing the remediation plan with references to ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-212, and ENG-219 with due dates. ENG-219 (Launch readiness checklist) -- no direct completion comment found; still showing as To Do. This is a read/lookup action.
OE 3: Search Slack messages for completion signals on prerequisite tickets not yet reflected in Linear status. Use conversations_search_messages (search_query: "ENG-214" or "config fix" or "pipeline" or "deploy" or similar) to find Slack messages in #engineering (channel C003) confirming work completion. Discover: Dmitri's comprehensive audit results and duplicate receipt detection findings posted in #engineering (ts 1775565000, April 7); Samira's confirmation in #engineering that auto-decisions were disabled and remediation plan is in progress; Lena's message in #engineering (ts 1775566020, April 7) confirming she will incorporate Dmitri's schema validation tests into the ENG-217 pipeline deployment gate (forward-looking commitment, not a completion signal for ENG-217 itself); Mina's Slack message in #engineering (ts 1775565600, April 7) stating intent to manually approve Maria Santos, James Okafor, and Lisa Park, but no follow-up confirmation of completion is found -- consistent with OE 10's finding that these records have not been corrected to "Approved" in Airtable. This is a read/lookup action.
OE 4: Search emails for completion signals on prerequisite tickets. Use search_emails (query: "config" or "pipeline" or "audit" or "readiness" or similar) to find emails confirming work done. Discover: Samira's email to Elena confirming auto-approve/deny disabled as of 10:45 AM on 3/18; Samira's email with the full audit summary (47 expenses, 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals); Samira's email to Marcus with the complete audit spreadsheet and $885 financial exposure figure. This is a read/lookup action.
OE 5: For prerequisite tickets where completion evidence is found in Slack/email/Linear comments but the Linear issue status is not updated, update the Linear issue status and add a comment. Use linear_update_issue (id: issue ID, state: "state_done" or similar) and linear_create_comment (issueId: issue ID, body: noting the completion evidence found). Tickets that need status updates include ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, and ENG-212 where work appears complete based on Linear comment and Slack/email evidence but Linear status still shows "In Progress." This is a write action.
OE 6: For any prerequisite tickets that are genuinely blocked or still incomplete with no completion signal, add a comment asking the assignee for an update. Use linear_create_comment (issueId: issue ID, body: requesting a status update). ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, assigned to Samira Tariq, state: To Do) has no completion evidence and should receive such a comment. This is a write action.
OE 7: Search Slack messages for anyone describing Dmitri's duplicate receipt detection work or the stipend audit as "fraud investigation" or "fraud detection." Use conversations_search_messages (search_query: "fraud" or "fraud investigation" or "fraud detection" or similar). Discover multiple instances of "fraud" language in Slack: Mina Hashimoto in #customer-engagement (channel C002, ts 1776534600) references "Kevin Tran's stipend receipt flagged in the fraud audit" when summarizing Rachel Whitfield's complaint about the Vectral contract renewal; Marcus Thorne in #finance (channel C005, ts 1776405780) states "finance is keeping the blanket hold in place on all pending stipend claims until the fraud review is complete"; Mina Hashimoto in #finance (channel C005, ts 1776394860) references "full fraud review" when asking about releasing Simone Richter's $340 reimbursement; Marcus Thorne in #finance (channel C005, ts 1775597280) posts about stipend holds -- "Total potential loss if all three are fraudulent: $223." This is a read/lookup action.
OE 8: Search emails for "fraud" language related to the duplicate detection or stipend audit. Use search_emails (query: "fraud" or "fraud investigation" or "fraud detection" or similar). Discover: Rachel Whitfield (rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com) email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (email_id: email_rachel_discount_demand) describes Kevin Tran's receipt as "flagged as part of a fraud investigation" -- exact quote: "your finance team flagged one of Kevin Tran's stipend receipts -- an $89 keyboard purchase -- as part of a fraud investigation" -- this is client-facing and the most serious instance, confirming the mischaracterization reached Vectral's HR; David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com references "the Kevin Tran stipend receipt that was flagged in the fraud audit and turned out to be legitimate"; Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com) email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com states "Finance is holding all claims until the fraud review is complete." This is a read/lookup action.
OE 9: Search Linear ticket comments on Dmitri's duplicate detection ticket (ENG-220) and related expense categorizer tickets for "fraud" language. Use linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection") and linear_list_comments on other relevant issues (e.g., ENG-216, ENG-211). Check whether any comments describe the detection output as "fraud investigation" or "fraud detection." Dmitri's own comments and the ticket description use terms like "questionable receipt submissions," "duplicate receipt anomalies," and "forensic fingerprints" -- not "fraud investigation." Dmitri's Slack post in #engineering (ts 1775598300) uses "fraud/error" once in the intro but describes the system as "duplicate receipt detection" and "manual verification." This is a read/lookup action.
OE 10: Check Airtable stipend records for Vectral false rejections from the pilot audit. Use airtable_list_bases to find the base, then airtable_list_tables (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001") to find the Stipends table (tblStipends00001), then airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records to find all Vectral stipend records. Discover the 5 false rejections identified in the audit: (1) Danielle Osei -- $475 ergonomic chair -- Approval Status: "Approved" (corrected by Mina), (2) Kevin Tran -- $185 internet setup -- Approval Status: "Approved" (corrected by Mina), (3) Maria Santos -- $550 standing desk -- Approval Status: "Rejected" (recStip000000018) or "Pending Approval" (rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk) depending on record -- NOT corrected to Approved, still outstanding, (4) James Okafor -- $82 keyboard tray -- Approval Status: "Rejected" (recStip000000019) -- NOT corrected, still outstanding, (5) Lisa Park -- $130 mesh WiFi system -- Approval Status: "Rejected" (recStip000000020) or "Pending Approval" (rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi) depending on record -- NOT corrected to Approved, still outstanding. Three records (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park) have not been corrected to "Approved" despite being within Vectral's real policy limits and need to be flagged. This is a read/lookup action.
OE 11: Update Dmitri's duplicate detection ticket (ENG-220) with findings on the mischaracterization issue. Use linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection", body: documenting the "fraud" language findings -- who said what and where, noting that the system detects duplicate receipt hashes and flags for manual verification not fraud, and that Rachel Whitfield at Vectral HR was told this was a "fraud investigation" which is a serious relaunch blocker). Since the language is coming from inside the team (David Chen, Marcus Thorne, and Mina Hashimoto all used "fraud" language internally and it reached the client), flag the ticket as a relaunch blocker by updating the issue priority or adding a label. Use linear_update_issue (id: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection", labels: ["relaunch-blocker"] or priority change or similar). This is a write action.
OE 12: Create a new Linear ticket under the expense categorizer project to track the uncorrected Vectral false rejections. Use linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering", title: related to uncorrected Vectral false rejections or similar, description: listing the 3 still-uncorrected records -- Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi -- noting these are system errors within Vectral's real policy limits that have not been corrected to Approved and need manual correction before relaunch, project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer", assignee: "moveops_dmitri_volkov"). This is a write action.
OE 13: Post a status summary in the #engineering Slack channel covering where each prerequisite stands and what blockers remain. Use conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: summary covering the status of each prerequisite ticket -- ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-219, ENG-212, ENG-216 -- noting which are complete, which are still in progress, the "fraud" language mischaracterization as a relaunch blocker, and the 3 uncorrected Vectral false rejections). This is a write action.
OE 14: Send Samira Tariq a heads-up about what's outstanding for the readiness checklist. Use conversations_add_message (to Samira's DM or in #engineering tagging her) or send_email (sender: "dmitri.volkov@moveops.com", recipients: ["samira.tariq@moveops.com"]) with a summary of outstanding items: the "fraud" language issue as a relaunch blocker, the 3 uncorrected false rejections, and the status of ENG-219 (her launch readiness checklist still in To Do). This is a write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for issues under the expense categorizer project to identify prerequisite tickets. Use linear_list_projects (query: "expense" or "categorizer" or similar) to find the project "Expense Auto-Categorization Engine" (project ID: proj_expense_autocategorizer). Then use linear_list_issues (project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or similar) to retrieve all tickets under this project. Discover the following prerequisite fix tickets relevant to relaunch readiness: ENG-214 ("[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config," assigned to Lena Björkström, state: In Progress), ENG-215 ("[P1] Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config," assigned to Lena Björkström, state: In Progress), ENG-217 ("[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline," assigned to Lena Björkström, state: In Progress), ENG-218 ("Dashboard: Add type validation to policy config editor," assigned to Anh Nguyen, state: In Progress), ENG-219 ("Define launch readiness checklist," assigned to Samira Tariq, state: To Do), ENG-212 ("Remaining edge case coverage," assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state: In Progress), and ENG-216 ("[URGENT] Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses," assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state: In Progress). This is a read/lookup action.
OE 2: Read comments on the prerequisite Linear tickets to check for completion signals. Use linear_list_comments (issueId for each relevant issue) to read comments on ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-219, ENG-212, and ENG-216. Discover completion evidence in comments: ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) -- Lena's deploy verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified, attached to ENG-215 / issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) explicitly states "Closing ENG-214" and confirms config corrections deployed and verified in production for both clients; the completion evidence for ENG-214 is found cross-referenced in ENG-215's comment thread rather than on ENG-214 directly. ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) -- Lena's deploy verification comment confirms config corrections deployed and verified: Danielle Osei's chair correctly approved, Andre Washington's desk correctly denied, coworking correctly denied. ENG-217 (Pipeline hardening) -- Dmitri's audit results comment (comment_dmitri_audit_results) is posted on this ticket (47 expenses, 39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 total financial exposure); this confirms the audit (ENG-216) is complete but does not itself signal that the pipeline hardening work is done -- no separate completion comment for ENG-217's pipeline fixes is found. ENG-218 (Dashboard type validation) -- no completion comment found on this ticket; still showing as In Progress with no evidence of completion. ENG-212 (Edge cases) -- Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_validation_tests) confirming 6 schema validation tests implemented and passing with corrected configs (test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback); also notes that Lena is incorporating these tests into the ENG-217 pipeline deployment gate. ENG-211 (Pilot monitoring) -- Samira's comment (comment_samira_pause_confirmed) listing the remediation plan with references to ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-212, and ENG-219 with due dates. ENG-219 (Launch readiness checklist) -- no direct completion comment found; still showing as To Do. This is a read/lookup action.
OE 3: Search Slack messages for completion signals on prerequisite tickets not yet reflected in Linear status. Use conversations_search_messages (search_query: "ENG-214" or "config fix" or "pipeline" or "deploy" or similar) to find Slack messages in #engineering (channel C003) confirming work completion. Discover: Dmitri's comprehensive audit results and duplicate receipt detection findings posted in #engineering (ts 1775565000, April 7); Samira's confirmation in #engineering that auto-decisions were disabled and remediation plan is in progress; Lena's message in #engineering (ts 1775566020, April 7) confirming she will incorporate Dmitri's schema validation tests into the ENG-217 pipeline deployment gate (forward-looking commitment, not a completion signal for ENG-217 itself); Mina's Slack message in #engineering (ts 1775565600, April 7) stating intent to manually approve Maria Santos, James Okafor, and Lisa Park, but no follow-up confirmation of completion is found -- consistent with OE 10's finding that these records have not been corrected to "Approved" in Airtable. This is a read/lookup action.
OE 4: Search emails for completion signals on prerequisite tickets. Use search_emails (query: "config" or "pipeline" or "audit" or "readiness" or similar) to find emails confirming work done. This is a read/lookup action.
OE 5: For prerequisite tickets where completion evidence is found in Slack/email/Linear comments but the Linear issue status is not updated, update the Linear issue status and add a comment. Tickets that need status updates include ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, and ENG-212 where work appears complete based on Linear comment and Slack/email evidence but Linear status still shows "In Progress." OE 6: For any prerequisite tickets that are genuinely blocked or still incomplete with no completion signal, add a comment asking the assignee for an update. OE 10: Check Airtable stipend records for Vectral false rejections from the pilot audit. Use airtable_list_bases to find the base, then airtable_list_tables (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001") to find the Stipends table (tblStipends00001), then airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records to find all Vectral stipend records. Discover the 5 false rejections identified in the audit: (1) Danielle Osei -- $475 ergonomic chair -- Approval Status: "Approved" (corrected by Mina), (2) Kevin Tran -- $185 internet setup -- Approval Status: "Approved" (corrected by Mina), (3) Maria Santos -- $550 standing desk -- Approval Status: "Rejected" (recStip000000018) or "Pending Approval" (rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk) depending on record -- NOT corrected to Approved, still outstanding, (4) James Okafor -- $82 keyboard tray -- Approval Status: "Rejected" (recStip000000019) -- NOT corrected, still outstanding, (5) Lisa Park -- $130 mesh WiFi system -- Approval Status: "Rejected" (recStip000000020) or "Pending Approval" (rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi) depending on record -- NOT corrected to Approved, still outstanding. Three records (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park) have not been corrected to "Approved" despite being within Vectral's real policy limits and need to be flagged. This is a read/lookup action.
OE 11: Update Dmitri's duplicate detection ticket (ENG-220) with findings on the mischaracterization issue. Use linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection", body: documenting the "fraud" language findings -- who said what and where, noting that the system detects duplicate receipt hashes and flags for manual verification not fraud, and that Rachel Whitfield at Vectral HR was told this was a "fraud investigation" which is a serious relaunch blocker). Since the language is coming from inside the team (David Chen, Marcus Thorne, and Mina Hashimoto all used "fraud" language internally and it reached the client), flag the ticket as a relaunch blocker by updating the issue priority or adding a label. Use linear_update_issue (id: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection", labels: ["relaunch-blocker"] or priority change or similar). This is a write action.
OE 12: Create a new Linear ticket under the expense categorizer project to track the uncorrected Vectral false rejections. Use linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering", title: related to uncorrected Vectral false rejections or similar, description: listing the 3 still-uncorrected records -- Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi -- noting these are system errors within Vectral's real policy limits that have not been corrected to Approved and need manual correction before relaunch, project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer", assignee: "moveops_dmitri_volkov"). This is a write action.
OE 13: Post a status summary in the #engineering Slack channel covering where each prerequisite stands and what blockers remain. Use conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: summary covering the status of each prerequisite ticket -- ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-219, ENG-212, ENG-216 -- noting which are complete, which are still in progress, the "fraud" language mischaracterization as a relaunch blocker, and the 3 uncorrected Vectral false rejections). This is a write action.
OE 14: Send Samira Tariq a heads-up about what's outstanding for the readiness checklist. Use conversations_add_message (to Samira's DM or in #engineering tagging her) or send_email (sender: "dmitri.volkov@moveops.com", recipients: ["samira.tariq@moveops.com"]) with a summary of outstanding items: the "fraud" language issue as a relaunch blocker, the 3 uncorrected false rejections, and the status of ENG-219 (her launch readiness checklist still in To Do). This is a write action.
